title: Retire or contain legacy non-Rust CKC/Posekit surfaces after native parity
operator_request: Address the non-Rust language things first, but do not delete useful behavior before native Handshake parity exists.

state_recovery.recovery_rule: Use MT-001 inventory. Only retire/contain a non-Rust surface when a native Rust/Handshake path is proven for the same behavior or the operator accepts deletion.

scope.summary: Finish the language-surface cleanup by retiring, containing, or documenting legacy React/TypeScript/JavaScript CKC/Posekit surfaces after native parity is proven.
base_scope: Re-open MT-001 inventory and verify which non-Rust surfaces remain live.
For each surface, classify as retired, contained, replaced by native, or intentionally retained.
Remove or route away from legacy surfaces only when native parity and tests exist.
Preserve useful behavior by moving it into native Handshake modules or explicit adapters.
Update internal UserManual and developer notes to point to native surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: Every MT-001 non-Rust CKC/Posekit surface has an updated disposition.
No useful production behavior is deleted without native replacement or explicit accepted non-goal.
Runtime navigation points to native Handshake surfaces.
Argus can inspect the final native entry points.
verification: Targeted build/tests for removed or rerouted surfaces.
Argus final inspection of native entry points.
